text.title: pytest Project Setup — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-pytest-setup application must obey.

rule.statement: Use pytest as the only test runner. Do not run unittest.main or nose for new code.
rule.rationale: Mixing runners means duplicated config and conflicting plugin ecosystems.

rule.statement: Set addopts = '--strict-markers' in pyproject. Declare every marker explicitly.
rule.rationale: Strict-markers turns typos in @pytest.mark.foo into errors instead of silently passing tests.

rule.statement: Structure tests/ to mirror src/ exactly. tests/<package>/test_<module>.py for src/<package>/<module>.py.
rule.rationale: Mirror layout makes discovery deterministic and removes ambiguity about which test covers which module.

rule.statement: Set --cov-fail-under threshold (≥80% branches for new code).
rule.rationale: Threshold turns coverage into a gate; otherwise it drifts down silently.

rule.statement: Use pytest-xdist (-n auto) in CI to parallelise tests across cores.
rule.rationale: Parallel execution halves CI time on multi-core runners; surfaces order-dependency bugs.

rule.statement: Follow the agentic TDD loop: write failing test → minimal code → refactor. Never write code before its test.
rule.rationale: Tests-after rationalise existing code; tests-first design the interface.
